title: Molten Norwegian Peanut Skillet
description: A rich and savory Scandinavian-inspired dish featuring roasted peanuts, bacon, and eggs, perfect for a hangover cure or a satisfying brunch. This skillet is low in calories but high in flavor, making it a great option for a weeknight dinner or weekend brunch.
instructions: Heat the butter in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the bacon and cook until crispy, about 6 minutes, then set aside.
In the same skillet, add the peanuts and cook for 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, heavy cream, salt, and pepper.
Pour the egg mixture over the peanuts in the skillet and cook until the edges start to set, about 2 minutes.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the eggs are almost set and the top is golden brown.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle the crispy bacon over the top.
Return the skillet to the stovetop and cook over medium heat for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the bacon is crispy and the eggs are fully set.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot, garnished with additional bacon and peanuts if desired.
Let the skillet rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the eggs to set completely.
